will be a standalone series of toonami will be episodic and not be serialized of other shows?

Whether a standalone series of Toonami would be episodic or serialized would depend on the creative direction and intentions of the show's creators. 

If the standalone series is meant to be episodic, then it would likely consist of standalone stories that don't heavily rely on continuous plotlines. This approach would be similar to the way in which many classic Toonami shows were structured, such as Gundam Wing and Dragon Ball Z. Each episode would be self-contained, with some overarching plot elements that get developed slowly over time but don't heavily rely on previous episodes.

On the other hand, if the standalone series were to be serialized, it would likely follow a more continuous story structure, with each episode contributing to a larger story arc. This approach would be similar to many modern Toonami shows, such as Attack on Titan and My Hero Academia. In this format, the episodes would be more tightly connected, and viewers would need to watch each one in order to fully understand the story.

Ultimately, the creative direction of a standalone series of Toonami would depend on the show's creators and their vision for the project. Whether it's episodic or serialized, the series would offer new and exciting storytelling opportunities for Toonami fans.
